title: When Interacting or Making Decisions: - Pause and Reflect:
subtitle: Spot and Challenge Hidden Biases
description:
When interacting or making decisions:
- Pause and reflect: "Am I acting on assumptions based on someone’s background or appearance?"
- Seek diverse input: Surround yourself with perspectives different from your own.
- Use structured decision-making: Avoid snap judgments by following a clear process.

Example: Hiring? Focus on qualifications and skills, not personal characteristics.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?

faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Track current decision-making processes for two weeks. Measure the number of decisions made, reversals, and the rationale behind them to establish a baseline for improvement.

howTo.title: Implement Pause-Checkpoint
howTo.summary: Add the Pause-Checkpoint to key decision-making moments. Use the 6-second pause to identify assumptions, gather evidence, and recognize missing perspectives before making decisions.

howTo.title: Log assumptions and actions
howTo.summary: Record assumptions and follow-up actions in the Brali LifeOS app after each Pause-Checkpoint. This creates accountability and helps track the impact of the practice.

howTo.title: Review and adjust
howTo.summary: After a month, review the logged data to assess the effectiveness of the Pause-Checkpoint. Adjust the process based on feedback and observed outcomes.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and insights in the Brali LifeOS app to track progress and reinforce the habit of checking assumptions during decision-making. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/bias-checkpoints-for-teams/.
